summary: DHH celebrates Rails reaching 1,000 downloads in its first two weeks after release, posting from vacation via a slow GPRS connection. He expresses excitement about the framework's early adoption and the positive feedback it has received. He shares a testimonial from a C++ systems programmer who discovered Rails while looking to offshore a CRM project. The post captures a pivotal early moment in Rails history, when the framework that would reshape web development was just getting its first traction.
key_points: Rails achieved 1,000 downloads within two weeks of its initial public release in July 2004
Early adopters included experienced systems programmers outside the typical web development audience
The framework was already generating enthusiastic testimonials from unexpected demographics
DHH was so excited about the milestone he posted from vacation on a 2kb/sec mobile connection
money_quotes: In the first two weeks since release, Rails has been downloaded more than 1,000 times.
I'm extremely pleased with its sweeping uptake and with all the lavish praise it has gathered already.
I write systems code (C++, database kernels, app servers, http proxies, etc.) for a living, so web development is not usually my cup of tea.
key_insight: A historical snapshot of Rails' earliest days, when 1,000 downloads felt like a remarkable milestone for the framework that would go on to power some of the internet's biggest applications.
